message.content:
I'll start by exploring the workspace structure to understand the current state and prior constraints. Let me read the available episodes and evidence. <function_calls>
<invoke name="shell">
<parameter name="command">find /tmp_workspace -type f -name "*.json" -o -name "*.md" -o -name "*.csv" -o -name "*.txt" 2>/dev/null | head -50</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls> <function_calls>
<invoke name="shell">
<parameter name="command">ls -la /tmp_workspace/</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls> <function_calls>
<invoke name="shell">
<parameter name="command">ls -la /tmp_workspace/episodes/ 2>/dev/null || echo "episodes dir not found"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls> <function_calls>
<invoke name="shell">
<parameter name="command">ls -la /tmp_workspace/evidence/ 2>/dev/null || echo "evidence dir not found"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to complete a multi-episode workflow. I need to:

1. Read prior constraints from /tmp_workspace/episodes/ and /tmp_workspace/evidence/
2. Continue from the latest state
3. Resolve source conflicts with explicit evidence priority
4. Produce three artifacts in /tmp_workspace/results/:
   - result.json
   - summary.md
   - manifest.csv

Let me start by exploring the workspace structure to understand what exists.
